fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 37-year-old Asian American male with schizoaffective disorder, bipolar type, presenting for medication monitoring.
fields.Subjective: Mr. Kim presents today reporting overall stability with some residual symptoms. He states he has been diligent about taking his ziprasidone 80 mg twice daily with meals as directed and uses reminders on his phone to maintain the schedule. He notes some improvement in mood compared to last month, though still experiences mild mood fluctuations throughout the week. Denies current manic symptoms but reports occasional racing thoughts in the late afternoon that are manageable with distraction techniques. He continues to experience infrequent visual distortions, describing them as 'things looking wavy' for brief periods, which he finds odd but not frightening. Sleep has been relatively stable at 6-7 hours per night. Appetite is fair with stable weight at 165 lbs. He has maintained his part-time position at a library and finds the structured environment helpful. Denies current substance use though acknowledges past cannabis use which he stopped 8 months ago. Reports attending his DBT skills group weekly which he finds beneficial for emotional regulation. No current safety concerns, suicidal or homicidal ideation. (Assessed by Dr. Rachel Friedman, Clinical Psychologist)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Casually dressed, appropriate grooming, friendly demeanor
Mental_Status_Examination.Speech: Normal rate, clear articulation, occasionally tangential

fields.Current_Medications: Ziprasidone 80 mg PO BID with food

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: Metabolic panel within normal limits; prolactin slightly elevated at 22 ng/mL.

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Schizoaffective disorder, bipolar type, stable with residual symptoms.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives independently; employed part-time at library; attends weekly DBT group; maintained sobriety from cannabis for 8 months.
Assessment_and_Plan: Continue ziprasidone 80 mg PO BID, patient adherent with phone reminder system.
Monitor mood fluctuations and racing thoughts; patient using adaptive coping strategies.
Reinforce continued participation in DBT skills group.
Recheck prolactin level at next visit.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Rachel Friedman, PhD, Clinical Psychologist — Integrated Behavioral Health Services, 3400 Broadway, Seattle, WA 98122, Phone: (555) 456-7890, Fax: (555) 456-7891
